description: PM framework v7.6 — Mechanical Enforcement. Promotes 4 silent agent-attention checks to write-time pre-commit failures (PHASE_TRANSITION_NO_LOG, PHASE_TRANSITION_NO_TIMING, BROKEN_PR_CITATION at write-time, CASE_STUDY_MISSING_TIER_TAGS), adds a per-PR review bot that fails the pm-framework/pr-integrity status check on new findings vs main, ships a weekly framework-status cron that watches for measurement-adoption regression, and explicitly enumerates the 5 mechanically unclosable Class B gaps. Builds on v7.5 (Data Integrity Framework, 8 cooperating defenses), v7.1 (72h integrity cycle), and v7.0 (HADF). Triggered by the 2026-04-21 Google Gemini 2.5 Pro independent audit; v7.5 closed the 9 Tier 1/2/3 items as policy and tooling, v7.6 closes the residual Class B → Class A gap by making the policy mechanically enforced.

audit_closures.FW-002: Description string updated from v5.2 to v7.0 — closed.
audit_closures.FW-007: chip-profiles.json verified at 17 profiles matching spec — see chip-profiles.json.
audit_closures.FW-008: hardware-signature-table.json verified at 7 signatures matching spec — see hadf/hardware-signature-table.json.
audit_closures.FW-009: HADF hardware_context settings verified (enabled=false, blend=0.4, trust=0.7) — see dispatch-intelligence.json hardware_context section.
audit_closures.FW-010: Skill routing entries verified against actual SKILL.md files — see skill-routing.json.
audit_closures.FW-013: Cache freshness check verified — referenced files exist for sampled cache entries.
audit_closures.FW-018: Chip Affinity Map empty by design (HADF Layer 4 not yet active) — see chip-affinity-map.json.

source_of_truth.branch_policy: treat main as authoritative until external systems are resynced

v7_1_integrity_cycle.description: 72-hour recurring audit of .claude/features/*/state.json. Catches 'shipped but unreconciled' drift within 72h of occurrence instead of weeks later.

v7_5_data_integrity_framework.description: Eight cooperating defenses catching broken data at write-time, cycle-time, and readout-time. Triggered by the 2026-04-21 Google Gemini 2.5 Pro independent audit.

tier_1_2_pr_resolution_write_time.enforcement: pre-commit hook blocks bogus phases.merge.pr_number on state.json write

tier_1_3_schema_enforcement_write_time.enforces: SCHEMA_DRIFT — rejects legacy `phase` key in favor of `current_phase`

workflow_hardening_2026_04_23: pipefail + PIPESTATUS[0] captures real integrity-check.py exit
snapshot_context metadata distinguishes scheduled_cycle from manual runs
regression detection uses grep-for-token on piped report

tier_3_3_external_replication.requires: independent human or AI operator running a feature through /pm-workflow

v7_6_mechanical_enforcement.description: Promotes 4 silent agent-attention checks to write-time pre-commit failures, adds per-PR + weekly CI defenses, and explicitly enumerates 5 mechanically unclosable Class B gaps. Closes the residual Class B → Class A gap left by v7.5.

phase_1a_phase_transition_no_log.rule: current_phase change in staged state.json must have a corresponding event in .claude/logs/<feature>.log.json within the last 15 minutes (PHASE_EVENT_FRESHNESS_MIN=15). Bypass for testing: FORCE_TRANSITION_CHECKS=1.

phase_1b_phase_transition_no_timing.rule: current_phase change in staged state.json must include timing.phases.<new_phase>.started_at and, when there was a previous phase, timing.phases.<old_phase>.ended_at in the same commit.

phase_1c_broken_pr_citation_write_time.rule: Pre-commit rejects any case-study commit citing PR #N or pull/N if the number does not resolve in the cached gh pr list result. Skipped gracefully when gh is unavailable; cycle-level BROKEN_PR_CITATION still runs as safety net.

phase_1d_case_study_missing_tier_tags.rule: Pre-commit rejects scoped post-2026-04-21 case study commits when the file contains no T1/T2/T3 tier tag at all. Presence-only; tag exhaustiveness/correctness remains a Class B review gap.

phase_2a_pr_review_bot.rule: Runs schema-check + integrity-check + measurement-adoption against PR HEAD. Captures origin/main baseline via worktree. Status = failure when any required command exits non-zero or when delta > 0 (PR introduces new findings vs main). Sticky comment with marker <!-- pm-framework-pr-integrity-bot --> updates in place on each push.

phase_2b_measurement_adoption_history.rule: Append-only dated snapshots. Dedup-by-date. Atomic write via temp file + rename. Unlocks Tier 1.1 trend mode after 3 snapshots accumulate.

phase_2c_weekly_framework_status_cron.regression_rule: fully_adopted decreased OR any_adopted decreased vs prior history snapshot
phase_2c_weekly_framework_status_cron.rule: Observational-only — never blocks merges. The 72h cycle owns blocking; this owns the weekly trend.

gaps.reason: Agent-attention — deciding what counts as a cache hit is itself the judgment we cannot mechanize.
Judgment-based magnitudes; we check presence, not whether 'novelty: 0.2' is the right number.
gaps.tracking: no issue — accepted as Class B by design

gaps.reason: Preflight checks tag PRESENCE on post-2026-04-21 case studies. Whether the tag is the RIGHT tag (T1 vs T2 vs T3) requires reading prose in context.
gaps.tracking: scripts/check-case-study-preflight.py for presence; correctness handled in code review

gaps.reason: Apple/Google sign-in handshake on a real device cannot be driven by an automated test runner without crossing into the mocking-pattern v7.5 was built to avoid.
No pre-commit hook can simulate 'an external operator on an unrelated product succeeded with the framework'.
gaps.tracking: Public GitHub issue (filed as the LAST v7.6 deliverable)
gaps.observability: docs/case-studies/external/ directory — created on first external submission

policy_decisions_continued: Publish-verbatim-then-remediate: every audit finding stays visible; corrections are appended.
Honest-status labels (partial / pilot / groundwork / backlog) over rounded-up ship-claims.
Tier 3.3 external invitation filed as the LAST v7.6 deliverable, only after every other piece is on main.

tooling_attribution.claude_opus_4_7: Framework rework, plan execution, code authoring, case study (Phase 1–4).
tooling_attribution.google_gemini_2_5_pro: Independent audit on 2026-04-21 that triggered v7.5 → v7.6 (different vendor, different model family, artifact-only access).
tooling_attribution.openai_codex: SSD audit on 2026-04-19 (docs/master-plan/codex-ssd-audit-2026-04-19.md). Identified the dashboard build break + SSD sprawl that motivated several pre-v7.5 hardening commits. Did not contribute v7.5/v7.6 commits per git log evidence; if additional Codex work in the v7.5+ window is identified later, this attribution will be appended (publish-verbatim policy).

skill_on_demand.description: Load only phase-relevant SKILL.md files instead of all 11. Saves ~30K tokens/session.

cache_compression.description: Each cache entry has a compressed_view (~200 words) loaded by default. Full expansion on demand. Saves ~24K tokens/session.

model_tiering.description: ANE mixed-precision analog: sonnet for mechanical phases, opus for judgment phases. Advisory tier recommendation per phase.

batch_dispatch.description: TPU weight-stationary analog: load template once, iterate N targets. 5x fewer dispatches for multi-target operations.

result_forwarding.description: UMA zero-copy analog: pass skill output inline to next skill. Eliminates write-to-disk-read-back serialization overhead.

speculative_preload.description: Branch prediction analog: pre-load likely-next-skill cache on skill start. 30-40% latency reduction on skill chains.
speculative_preload.inspiration: CPU branch prediction, validated by arxiv 2510.04371

systolic_chains.description: TPU systolic array analog: isolated pipeline execution. Each skill receives only upstream output + own L1 cache. No global reads mid-chain. Hybrid isolation: full for UX/design, partial for implementation.

task_complexity_gate.description: ARM big.LITTLE analog: classify tasks by complexity, route lightweight to parallel E-core lane (sonnet) and heavyweight to serial P-core lane (opus). Parallel-first execution clears backlog fast.

v5_soc_optimizations.combined_savings: ~54K tokens (items 1+2) + dispatch reduction (item 3) + serialization elimination (item 4) + cost optimization (item 5) + latency reduction (item 6) + bottleneck elimination (item 7) + complexity-aware dispatch (item 8)
